entries.proves: Runs the selected validation/proof command through the temporary env wrapper and cleans up .env.local when the wrapper created it.
entries.doesNotProve: Does not prove deployed provider behavior unless the selected command has deployed URL, live provider secrets, OAuth, and operator evidence inputs.
entries.failureHandling: Diagnostic/orchestration only; no independent pass claim.

entries.doesNotProve: Does not guarantee live providers unless live mode and restored env succeed; does not prove human visual/media/business approval.

entries.productionRisk: Lifecycle closure sequence could drift or become undiscoverable.
entries.proves: Provides the canonical repo-owned closure orchestrator or its wiring-only dry run.
entries.doesNotProve: Does not prove live deployed execution unless the non-dry-run command is run with all required inputs.
entries.failureHandling: Juniper repairs orchestration wiring and reruns.
entries.blockerPolicy: The live command is required for COMPLETE; the matrix entry itself is informational orchestration.

entries.productionRisk: A new operator could miss required lifecycle or cleanup commands.
entries.proves: Package, updater contract, README, authority index, and lifecycle runbook stay aligned.
entries.doesNotProve: Does not execute deployed lifecycle stages.
entries.failureHandling: Juniper repairs command/document wiring before release handoff.
entries.blockerPolicy: Strong warning; blocks lifecycle handoff quality, not application build.

entries.productionRisk: Cross-suite visual identity drifts or generic SaaS colors displace the West Peek brand.
entries.whatItProves: Canonical #F05A1A token, restrained orange rule, and brand authority exist.
entries.whatItDoesNotProve: Does not replace route-complete Hallmark visual review.
entries.failureHandling: Repair before Hallmark approval and COMPLETE status.

entries.blockerPolicy: Strong warning; required before Hallmark approval and COMPLETE status, but not a compile hard-fail.

entries.productionRisk: Updater-ready baseline may omit required executable inputs or include forbidden generated/runtime files.
entries.proves: Required root files and repo-specific packaging prerequisites exist; forbidden local/generated paths are absent.
entries.doesNotProve: Runtime behavior, browser behavior, deployment, or live providers.
entries.failureHandling: Repair the source artifact and repackage before updater execution.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks artifact delivery and updater execution.

entries.productionRisk: Final ZIP staging tree could include forbidden generated/runtime files if packaging validation is skipped.
entries.proves: Artifact-only required-file and exclusion checks.
entries.doesNotProve: Runtime or deployed behavior.
entries.failureHandling: Repair artifact and repackage.
entries.blockerPolicy: Packaging-only command; not part of installed-repo validate:all.

entries.productionRisk: Suite lifecycle contract drift could allow required release stages to disappear.
entries.proves: validate:suite-release-lifecycle-contract
entries.doesNotProve: Does not replace deployed provider/live proof.
entries.failureHandling: Repair source, rebuild full baseline, reopen, and rerun before delivery or push.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks delivery or push until proof passes.

entries.productionRisk: The owner updater could become the first environment to discover stale tests, missing files, or broken builds.
entries.proves: validate:reopened-baseline
entries.doesNotProve: Does not replace deployed provider/live proof.
entries.failureHandling: Repair source, rebuild full baseline, reopen, and rerun before delivery or push.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks delivery or push until proof passes.

entries.doesNotProve: Does not replace deployed provider/live proof.
entries.failureHandling: Repair source, rebuild full baseline, reopen, and rerun before delivery or push.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks delivery or push until proof passes.

entries.productionRisk: Commit or push could proceed without a passing desktop/mobile browser run tied to durable evidence.
entries.proves: Latest local browser proof passed, both declared projects met minimum collected counts, zero failures, and a local proof manifest exists.
entries.doesNotProve: Deployed runtime or live provider behavior.
entries.failureHandling: Block commit and push; repair source or browser environment and rerun.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks local prepush and updater commit/push.

entries.productionRisk: Applicable Pitch Lab runtime journeys or provider lanes could be omitted from lifecycle closure.
entries.proves: Pitch Lab has a complete repo-specific three-tier proof inventory with no Tier 4 testing tier.
entries.doesNotProve: The listed runtime proofs have executed.
entries.failureHandling: Repair the proof contract and lifecycle wiring before release.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks container prepush and packaging.

entries.productionRisk: A static build can render HTML while shared ESM dependencies are omitted, leaving every interactive journey unhydrated in a real browser.
entries.proves: All browser entry modules and their relative import graph exist in dist/assets after build.
entries.doesNotProve: Browser execution, user interaction correctness, deployed provider behavior, or visual quality.
entries.failureHandling: Repair the source build graph, rebuild, and rerun container plus local browser validation before commit.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks delivery, updater execution, commit, and push.

entries.productionRisk: Lifecycle closure can delete locally managed secrets, run deterministic tests against the deployed site, or skip deployed proof because equivalent URL aliases are not normalized.
entries.proves: Existing .env.local ownership is preserved, vault restore is fallback-only, local and deployed proof lanes are isolated, deploy URL aliases normalize, and browser concurrency is bounded.
entries.doesNotProve: Real provider credentials are configured, provider APIs succeed, or deployed Cloudflare secrets match the local environment.
entries.failureHandling: Repair environment ownership, URL normalization, or browser isolation before lifecycle closure and artifact delivery.
entries.blockerPolicy: Blocks local validation because destructive or ambiguous release environment handling can invalidate every later proof.
